title: [apiv2] can't build successfully an image from the given Dockerfile
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080

2. to create a Dockerfile 

3. curl -v -X POST "http://localhost:8080/v1.40/build?dockerfile=./Dockerfile"|jq

**Describe the results you received:**
```
1. can successfully build an image from Dockerfile by podman cmdline
[fedora@fedora32 containers]$ ./libpod/bin/podman build -f Dockerfile
STEP 1: FROM busybox
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d9cbbca60e5f done
Copying config 78096d0a54 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: COPY ./hello /hello
--> 09ed0df702c
STEP 3: RUN cat /hello
hello
STEP 4: COMMIT
--> a074a7c68b1
a074a7c68b17ce7cc941182b7a3833603a850f14243c34442c2ab693b0fdd68f

[fedora@fedora32 containers]$ ./libpod/bin/podman images|grep busybox
docker.io/library/busybox                 latest  78096d0a5478  5 days ago   1.44 MB

[fedora@fedora32 containers]$ ./libpod/bin/podman rmi busybox
Untagged: docker.io/library/busybox:latest

2. to build image w/ the same Dockerfile by curl
[fedora@fedora32 containers]$ curl -v -X POST "http://localhost:8080/v1.40/build?dockerfile=./Dockerfile"|jq
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/build?dockerfile=./Dockerfile HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Tue, 19 May 2020 11:27:18 GMT
< Content-Length: 506
<
{ [506 bytes data]
100   506  100   506    0     0   2032      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--  2024
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
{
  "cause": "no such file or directory",
  "message": "error building at STEP \"ADD /$SCRIPT_BASE $GOSRC/$SCRIPT_BASE\": no files found matching \"/tmp/libpod_builder367071067/build/contrib/cirrus\": no such file or directory",
  "response": 500
}
{
  "stream": "\nFROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest\nENV GOPATH=/var/tmp/go\nENV GOSRC=$GOPATH/src/github.com/containers/libpod\nENV SCRIPT_BASE=./contrib/cirrus\nENV PACKER_BASE=$SCRIPT_BASE/packer\nADD /$SCRIPT_BASE $GOSRC/$SCRIPT_BASE\nSuccessfully built \n"
}

NOTE: it still raised an error like 'no such file or directory' even though a absolute path is given for the Dockerfile, I also tried to use http encode to pass parameter to curl w/o double quota around URL, I got the same result. It is more puzzling that the registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest is pulled on the local.

[fedora@fedora32 containers]$ ./libpod/bin/podman images|grep busybox
[fedora@fedora32 containers]$ ./libpod/bin/podman images|grep fedora
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora         latest  eb7134a03cd6  4 days ago   208 MB
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
Can build successfully an image from the given Dockerfile
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &
[1] 109052
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ This function is EXPERIMENTAL.
http: superfluous response.WriteHeader call from github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/api/handlers/utils.WriteJSON (handler.go:64)
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         0f8ad039235137d086b9fea33bb74b362e625cdd
Built:              Tue May 19 11:02:16 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ git rev-parse HEAD
0f8ad039235137d086b9fea33bb74b362e625cdd
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Opentack VM

comments.body:
The usage of curl is not correct (see https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.40/#operation/ImageBuild). The build endpoint expects a tarfile with the Dockerfile(s) and the entire build context:
```
 --request POST \
 --header "Content-Type: application/x-tar" \
 --data-binary "@context.tar" \
```

I have to leave the keyboard in a bit, so I cannot reproduce but the usage of this issue is wrong and Podman validly errors out.

> 
> 
> The usage of curl is not correct (see https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.40/#operation/ImageBuild). The build endpoint expects a tarfile with the Dockerfile(s) and the entire build context:
> 
> ```
>  --request POST \
>  --header "Content-Type: application/x-tar" \
>  --data-binary "@context.tar" \
> ```
> 
Thanks, but I haven't found related documentation about 'header Parameters' in http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/buildImage
It would be great to get these curl commands into a test suite that we could run on each PR.

> > The usage of curl is not correct (see https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.40/#operation/ImageBuild). The build endpoint expects a tarfile with the Dockerfile(s) and the entire build context:
> > ```
> >  --request POST \
> >  --header "Content-Type: application/x-tar" \
> >  --data-binary "@context.tar" \
> > ```
> 
> Thanks, but I haven't found related documentation about 'header Parameters' in http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/buildImage

That's a fair point. The docs should mention that.

@vrothberg , I still can't get expected result w/ above parameters, maybe, I'm wrong, could you give a cmdline example?  thanks!

```
$ file busybox-latest.tar
busybox-latest.tar: POSIX tar archive

$ curl -v -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/x-tar" --data-binary "@busybox-latest.tar" http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/build|jq
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, POST is already inferred.
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current

{ [10 bytes data]

* Closing connection 0
parse error: Invalid numeric literal at line 1, column 4
```
@chuanchang You are getting back a 404, `v1.40/libpod/build` doesn't exist. You most likely ment `v1.40/build` instead since that are the docker api endpoints.
i also just made a change upstream so libpod/build now exists.

Both the /libpod/build and /build didn't work for me, if my cmdline is wrong please correct me, thanks.
